qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed pac4j session cookie can trigger pre-auth heap exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets `druid-pac4j` cookie parsing and `CryptoService` heap exhaustion before authentication, not the `druid-basic-security` LDAP empty-password anonymous-bind acceptance described by the advisory; its PoC was reproduced on Druid 36.0.0, the advisory's fixed version.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Status properties endpoint leaks secret-bearing configuration values
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns `/status/properties` filtering in `StatusResource` and disclosure of configuration secrets, not the advisory's LDAP authentication bypass in `druid-basic-security`; its PoC was also reproduced on Druid 36.0.0, the advisory's fixed version.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: LDAP authentication in `druid-basic-security` accepting an existing username with an empty password when the backing LDAP server permits anonymous bind.
evidence: GHSA-q672-hfc7-g833 describes an authentication bypass in the `druid-basic-security` LDAP authenticator that treats anonymous LDAP bind success as valid authentication for an existing username with an empty password.
`pac4j_malformed_session_cookie_dos` instead documents pre-auth heap exhaustion in `extensions-core/druid-pac4j` and `processing/.../CryptoService` during crafted pac4j session cookie decryption.
`status_properties_secret_exposure` instead documents secret disclosure in `server/.../StatusResource.getProperties` due to incomplete hidden-property filtering on `/status/properties`.

Both qualifying finding reports state their PoCs reproduced on Apache Druid 36.0.0 commit `6a88a829460664ec85cbc1425f148c1eb0849935`, while the advisory lists 36.0.0 as the fixed version for the LDAP authentication bypass.
